executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture ownership, live topology checks, and fail-closed verification are healthy; end-to-end green remains blocked by a marketing-owned Codeberg adoption measurement window.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Hold architecture at qualified pass while marketing waits out or replaces the current Codeberg-primary distribution tactic if the active review window stays flat.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and verifier refreshes
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing adoption window remains the only live blocker to full green
findings.mechanism: Marketing runner execution recovered, but marketing independent verification still fails closed while Codeberg adoption stays flat inside the active measurement window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for the marketing owner loop is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Do not certify around the watchpoint; wait for measurable Codeberg movement or a marketing-owned tactic replacement at the review window.

findings.title: Recovered marketing log-shape drift that was falsely reddening the runner bundle
findings.mechanism: Legacy marketing action logs stored channel as a string, which broke selector/audit normalization and created a local runner-bundle blocker unrelated to outcome health.
findings.root_cause: Normalization code assumed channel payloads were always dicts.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep legacy-log normalization tolerant of both dict and string channel shapes so measurement-pending marketing state does not masquerade as a runtime crash.

findings.title: Persisted disabled cron history still exists but is not a live-topology problem
findings.mechanism: jobs.json contains disabled historical entries while the live Gateway topology has zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Persisted history and live scheduler state are distinct surfaces and must stay reported separately.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live enabled runtime jobs in all architecture summaries.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture signoff tied to the external marketing blocker and not to derivative runner/verifier redness inside that owner loop
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The architecture path is healthy in the live runtime and should stay localized to the real blocker.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: No bogus architecture incident while the real blocker remains marketing-owned.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Resolve the marketing measurement window with measurable primary-repo movement or tactic replacement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Codeberg adoption is still flat across the active window.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either a marketing pass artifact or a concrete replacement path owned by marketing.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Patched distribution_lane_selector.py and marketing_workflow_audit.py to accept legacy string-valued channel payloads as well as dict payloads.
Reran marketing_loop_runner.py and marketing_workflow_audit.py; the runner bundle is operational again and only outcome certification remains red.
Refreshed loop_integrity_audit.py, health_monitor.py, and architecture verifier inputs against the current live Gateway topology.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms architecture-owned checks are green and the remaining blocker is marketing-owned.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass after primary-repo adoption moves or the current tactic is replaced at the end of the active measurement window.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Primary Codeberg adoption is still flat under the active marketing measurement window
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: That marketing-owned outcome gap is still the only blocker preventing a fully green end-to-end certification.
